exercises.explain: Deepfakes use advanced computer models to swap features like faces and voices, making imaginary scenarios look incredibly real.

options.text: Highly realistic fake videos or audio generated by AI
Photos edited with simple filter apps on your phone
Videos filmed with special green screens in movie studios

cards.title: Did They Really Say That?
cards.body: Have you ever seen a video of a famous actor or president saying something completely wild, only to find out they never actually said it? Welcome to the era of deepfakes, where AI makes the impossible look real!

cards.title: Defining Deepfakes
cards.body: A deepfake is synthetic media—like videos, images, or audio—created by powerful generative AI models (such as StyleGAN) that realistically swap faces, alter expressions, or mimic voices.

Think of a deepfake like a digital costume party. Instead of putting on a physical mask, the computer paints a brand new, highly realistic digital mask over someone's face, blending it perfectly with their real movements.
cards.title: The Digital Mask

cards.body: Imagine a filmmaker using StyleGAN to generate a background crowd of people who don't actually exist, or swapping an actor's face with their stunt double to make an action sequence seamless.
cards.title: Hollywood Magic

cards.body: Myth: You need a multimillion-dollar studio to make a deepfake. Fact: While professional tools are advanced, user-friendly AI apps now allow almost anyone with a smartphone to swap faces in seconds.
cards.title: Only for Experts?
Comparing the Tech

right.text: Using StyleGAN to blend facial features, matching the original lighting, shadows, and expressions perfectly.

left.text: Using Photoshop to crop and paste a face onto another body. The lighting and edges often look blocky or unnatural.

cards.body: Let's compare a traditional photo edit with an AI-generated deepfake.

exercises.explain: Generative models like StyleGAN analyze real facial expressions and reconstruct them on a different target face with realistic details.

options.text: By cutting and pasting static photos together like a collage
By using generative models to synthesize and blend realistic facial features
By applying green screen paint to the actor's face manually

exercises.prompt: How do generative models create deepfakes?
True or False: StyleGAN is a generative model often used to create realistic synthetic media.
exercises.explain: StyleGAN is a state-of-the-art generative network famous for generating highly realistic synthetic faces.

exercises.sentence: Deepfakes are a form of ________ media created by generative models.
exercises.explain: Deepfakes generate synthetic media, meaning the content is simulated by AI rather than recorded from real life.
exercises.prompt: Complete the sentence to define deepfake content:

options.text: A static picture with a cartoon filter applied over the eyes
A video where a celebrity's face matches the exact speech and head movements of a voice actor
A simple hand-drawn animation of a talking dog

exercises.explain: AI-generated deepfakes can match subtle details like lighting, skin texture, and mouth movements, making them look authentic to human viewers.
exercises.prompt: Which of the following describes a generative AI deepfake?
Which of the following is true about modern deepfakes generated by models like StyleGAN?

options.text: They are highly convincing and can appear completely authentic to humans.
They are always blurry and have watermark logos on them.
They can only be viewed in black and white format.

exercises.explain: Generative models have advanced rapidly, making synthetic media look authentic. In the next lesson, we will explore the tools used to detect these deepfakes!

title: The Rise of Deepfakes
concept: Deepfakes use generative models (like StyleGAN) to swap faces or generate synthetic media that looks completely authentic to human viewers.
